title: How Elections Work — A Step-by-Step Guide

steps.title: Voter Registration
steps.description: Before you can vote, you must register with your local election authority. This confirms your identity, age, citizenship, and residency.
details: Check if your state or country has automatic voter registration
Register online, by mail, or in person at designated offices
Note the registration deadline — some regions require registration weeks before election day
Keep your registration updated if you move or change your name

steps.title: Candidate Nominations
steps.description: Candidates declare their intent to run and go through a nomination process within their party or as independents.
details: Parties hold primaries or caucuses to select their nominees
Independent candidates may need to collect petition signatures
Candidates file official paperwork and financial disclosures
Nomination deadlines vary by jurisdiction and office

steps.title: Campaign Period
steps.description: Candidates engage with voters through rallies, debates, advertising, and door-to-door canvassing to share their platforms.
details: Campaign finance laws regulate fundraising and spending
Debates give voters a chance to compare candidates directly
Media coverage and fact-checking help inform the electorate
Campaigns must follow advertising disclosure rules

steps.title: Early & Absentee Voting
steps.description: Many jurisdictions offer early voting or absentee/mail-in ballots for voters who cannot attend on election day.
details: Check if your jurisdiction offers early in-person voting
Request an absentee ballot well before the deadline
Follow all instructions carefully when completing a mail-in ballot
Track your ballot status online if available

steps.title: Election Day
steps.description: Registered voters cast their ballots at designated polling stations or through other approved methods.
details: Locate your assigned polling station in advance
Bring required identification documents
Polls are open during set hours — plan to arrive with time to spare
Poll workers are available to assist with any questions

steps.title: Vote Counting & Results
steps.description: After polls close, election officials count ballots using secure, auditable processes to determine the winners.
details: Counting methods vary: hand-counted paper ballots, optical scanners, or electronic systems
Observers from parties and independent groups monitor the count
Preliminary results may be available on election night
Official certification takes days or weeks depending on the jurisdiction

steps.title: Certification & Transition
steps.description: Election results are officially certified and the winning candidates prepare to take office.
details: Canvassing boards verify and certify the final vote totals
Candidates may challenge results through legal processes if warranted
A peaceful transition of power follows certification
Newly elected officials are inaugurated on the designated date
